# Urban Loggers LLC
## Tree Service in Greater Milwaukee, WI

> Urban Loggers LLC provides professional tree removal, trimming, pruning, stump grinding, emergency tree service, and portable log milling throughout Greater Milwaukee, Wisconsin. Owner Brian Smith has 20+ years of experience and the company is fully insured.

## Services
- Tree Removal (any size, near structures, rigging available)
- Tree Trimming & Pruning (crown thinning, deadwood, structural pruning, orchard pruning)
- Stump Grinding (below grade, multi-stump discounts)
- Emergency Tree Service (24/7 storm response, fallen trees, power line hazards)
- Log Milling (portable sawmill — live-edge slabs, beams, dimensional lumber, custom cuts)

## Service Area
Greater Milwaukee, Wisconsin including:
- Milwaukee County: Milwaukee, Wauwatosa, West Allis, Greenfield, South Milwaukee
- Waukesha County: Waukesha, Brookfield, New Berlin, Pewaukee
- Racine County: Racine, Mount Pleasant
- Ozaukee County: Mequon, Cedarburg, Port Washington

## Contact
- Phone: (414) 514-0750
- Email: urbanloggersllc@gmail.com
- Website: https://urbanloggers.org

## Key Facts
- 20+ years experience (since 2003)
- Fully insured (liability + workers comp)
- Free on-site estimates
- 24/7 emergency response
- Portable sawmill — trees milled into usable lumber on-site
- Specialty pruning: orchard, pollarding, European espalier

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q: How much does tree removal cost in Milwaukee?
A: Most Milwaukee tree removals range from $300 to $2,000+ depending on size, access, hazards, and whether stump grinding is included.

Q: Do I need a permit to remove a tree in Milwaukee?
A: Milwaukee requires a permit to remove trees larger than 12 inches DBH on public property. Private property trees generally don't require a permit. Urban Loggers handles permit applications.

Q: Do you offer emergency tree removal?
A: Yes. Urban Loggers LLC responds 24/7 for fallen trees, storm damage, and hazardous limbs throughout Greater Milwaukee.

Q: What is log milling?
A: Urban Loggers brings a portable sawmill to your property and mills felled logs into usable lumber — live-edge slabs, dimensional boards, fireplace mantels, and custom beams.

Q: When is the best time to prune trees in Milwaukee?
A: Late winter to early spring (February-March) is ideal for most species. Dormant pruning reduces disease risk and allows spring growth to heal wounds quickly.

Q: Are you insured?
A: Yes. Urban Loggers LLC carries full liability insurance and workers' compensation coverage for every job.
